```
├── Controllers/
│   ├── IngestController.cs      # POST /ingest
│   └── AskController.cs         # POST /ask
├── Models/
│   ├── DocumentChunk.cs         # Vector store record
│   ├── AskRequest.cs            # { question }
│   └── AskResponse.cs           # { answer, sources[] }
├── Services/
│   ├── IngestionService.cs      # Chunking + embedding + upsert
│   └── QueryService.cs          # Retrieve + prompt + LLM call
├── Options/
│   └── AiOptions.cs             # Configuration POCO
├── Docs/
│   ├── configuration.md
│   └── getting-started.md
├── Program.cs                   # DI registration
└── ProductQA.csproj
```

### 8. Future enhancements (out of scope for v1)

- Swap in-memory vector store for Qdrant/pgvector in production
- Streaming responses via `IAsyncEnumerable` for lower time-to-first-token
- File-watcher to auto-re-ingest when docs change
- Semantic caching for repeated/similar questions
- Evaluation harness with golden Q&A dataset to measure answer quality over time

# RAG-Based Q&A for ProductQA Web API

## Problem

The ProductQA .NET 10 Web API has product documentation markdown files in `Docs/`. Users need an API to ask natural-language questions and receive answers grounded in those documents. This requires a document ingestion pipeline (chunking + embedding + storage) and a query endpoint (retrieval + LLM-grounded response).

## Technology Selection (per decision tree)

| Concern | Technology | Package |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Abstraction layer | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` 9.x |
| LLM provider | Azure OpenAI or OpenAI | `Azure.AI.OpenAI` 2.x or `OpenAI` 2.x |
| Embeddings | MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` | (included in provider SDK) |
| Vector store | MEVD abstraction + in-memory or Qdrant | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` 9.x + provider package |
| Document ingestion & chunking | DataIngestion preview | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` 9.x-preview |
| Token counting | ML.Tokenizers | `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` 2.x |

**Why NOT Agent Framework?** This is a single prompt→response flow (user question → retrieve chunks → generate answer). No tool calling, agentic loops, or multi-step reasoning is needed. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.

# RAG-Based Q&A for ProductQA Web API

## Problem

The ProductQA .NET 10 Web API has product documentation markdown files in `Docs/`. Users need an API to ask natural-language questions and receive answers grounded in those documents. This requires a document ingestion pipeline (chunking + embedding + storage) and a query endpoint (retrieval + LLM-grounded response).

## Technology Selection (per decision tree)

| Concern | Technology | Package |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Abstraction layer | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` 9.x |
| LLM provider | Azure OpenAI or OpenAI | `Azure.AI.OpenAI` 2.x or `OpenAI` 2.x |
| Embeddings | MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` | (included in provider SDK) |
| Vector store | MEVD abstraction + in-memory or Qdrant | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` 9.x + provider package |
| Document ingestion & chunking | DataIngestion preview | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` 9.x-preview |
| Token counting | ML.Tokenizers | `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` 2.x |

**Why NOT Agent Framework?** This is a single prompt→response flow (user question → retrieve chunks → generate answer). No tool calling, agentic loops, or multi-step reasoning is needed. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Docs/*.md   │       │            ProductQA Web API                 │
│  (on disk)   │       │                                              │
└──────┬───────┘       │  ┌────────────┐      ┌───────────────────┐  │

       │               │  └────────────┘      │  • Chunk by §/¶   │  │
       │               │                      │  • Generate embeds │  │
       │               │                      │  • Upsert to store │  │
       │               │                      └────────┬──────────┘  │
       │               │                               │             │
       │               │                      ┌────────▼──────────┐  │
       │               │                      │   Vector Store    │  │
       │               │                      │  (in-memory or    │  │
       │               │                      │   Qdrant/pgvector)│  │
       │               │                      └────────▲──────────┘  │
       │               │                               │             │
       │               │  ┌────────────┐      ┌────────┴──────────┐  │

       │               │                      └───────────────────┘  │
```

  - `Key` (string, unique ID)

  - `Source` (string, original file path)
  - `Title` (string, document heading)
- Annotate with `[VectorStoreRecordKey]`, `[VectorStoreRecordData]`, `[VectorStoreRecordVector(Dimensions)]`.

  - Inject retrieved chunks as context with source attribution.
  - User message: the original question.
- Call `IChatClient.GetResponseAsync()` with `Temperature = 0f`, `MaxOutputTokens = 1024`.

| Embedding caching | Store embeddings in vector store; skip re-embedding on re-ingest if content unchanged |

### 7. Project structure (new files)

```
ProductQA/
├── Controllers/
│   ├── IngestController.cs      # POST /ingest
│   └── AskController.cs         # POST /ask
├── Models/
│   ├── DocumentChunk.cs         # Vector store record
│   ├── AskRequest.cs            # { question }
│   └── AskResponse.cs           # { answer, sources[] }
├── Services/
│   ├── IngestionService.cs      # Chunking + embedding + upsert
│   └── QueryService.cs          # Retrieve + prompt + LLM call
├── Options/
│   └── AiOptions.cs             # Configuration POCO
├── Docs/
│   ├── configuration.md
│   └── getting-started.md
├── Program.cs                   # DI registration
└── ProductQA.csproj
```

### 8. Future enhancements (out of scope for v1)

- Swap in-memory vector store for Qdrant/pgvector in production
- Streaming responses via `IAsyncEnumerable` for lower time-to-first-token
- File-watcher to auto-re-ingest when docs change
- Semantic caching for repeated/similar questions
- Evaluation harness with golden Q&A dataset to measure answer quality over time

## Notes

- The in-memory vector store is fine for dev/testing with a small doc set (2 files currently). Production should use a persistent store.
- This is explicitly NOT an agentic scenario — no Microsoft.Agents.AI needed.
- All services go through MEAI abstractions — no direct provider SDK calls in business logic.

## Technology Selection (per decision tree)

| Abstraction layer | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` 9.x |
| LLM provider | Azure OpenAI or OpenAI | `Azure.AI.OpenAI` 2.x or `OpenAI` 2.x |
| Embeddings | MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` | (included in provider SDK) |
| Vector store | MEVD abstraction + in-memory or Qdrant | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` 9.x + provider package |
| Document ingestion & chunking | DataIngestion preview | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` 9.x-preview |
| Token counting | ML.Tokenizers | `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` 2.x |

**Why NOT Agent Framework?** This is a single prompt→response flow (user question → retrieve chunks → generate answer). No tool calling, agentic loops, or multi-step reasoning is needed. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Docs/*.md   │       │            ProductQA Web API                 │
│  (on disk)   │       │                                              │

       │               │                               │             │
```

# RAG-Based Q&A for ProductQA Web API

## Problem

The ProductQA .NET 10 Web API has product documentation markdown files in `Docs/`. Users need an API to ask natural-language questions and receive answers grounded in those documents. This requires a document ingestion pipeline (chunking + embedding + storage) and a query endpoint (retrieval + LLM-grounded response).

## Technology Selection (per decision tree)

| Concern | Technology | Package |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Abstraction layer | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` 9.x |
| LLM provider | Azure OpenAI or OpenAI | `Azure.AI.OpenAI` 2.x or `OpenAI` 2.x |
| Embeddings | MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` | (included in provider SDK) |
| Vector store | MEVD abstraction + in-memory or Qdrant | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` 9.x + provider package |
| Document ingestion & chunking | DataIngestion preview | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` 9.x-preview |
| Token counting | ML.Tokenizers | `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` 2.x |

**Why NOT Agent Framework?** This is a single prompt→response flow (user question → retrieve chunks → generate answer). No tool calling, agentic loops, or multi-step reasoning is needed. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Docs/*.md   │       │            ProductQA Web API                 │
│  (on disk)   │       │                                              │
└──────┬───────┘       │  ┌────────────┐      ┌───────────────────┐  │
       │               │  │ POST       │      │ IngestionService  │  │
       │               │  │ /ingest    │─────▶│  • Read .md files │  │
       │               │  └────────────┘      │  • Chunk by §/¶   │  │
       │               │                      │  • Generate embeds │  │
       │               │                      │  • Upsert to store │  │
       │               │                      └────────┬──────────┘  │
       │               │                               │             │
       │               │                      ┌────────▼──────────┐  │
       │               │                      │   Vector Store    │  │
       │               │                      │  (in-memory or    │  │
       │               │                      │   Qdrant/pgvector)│  │
       │               │                      └────────▲──────────┘  │
       │               │                               │             │
       │               │  ┌────────────┐      ┌────────┴──────────┐  │
       │               │  │ POST       │      │  QueryService     │  │
       │               │  │ /ask       │─────▶│  • Embed question │  │
       │               │  └────────────┘      │  • Vector search  │  │
       │               │                      │  • Build prompt   │  │
       │               │                      │  • Call LLM       │  │
       │               │                      │  • Return answer  │  │
       │               │                      │   + sources       │  │
       │               │                      └───────────────────┘  │
       │               └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add NuGet packages

- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (9.x) — `IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- `Azure.AI.OpenAI` (2.x) or `OpenAI` (2.x) — concrete provider
- `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` (9.x) — `IVectorStore`, `VectorStoreCollection`
- Vector store provider package (e.g., `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.InMemory` for dev, Qdrant/pgvector for prod)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` (9.x-preview) — document parsing, chunking, embedding pipeline
- `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` (2.x) — client-side token counting for cost control

### 2. Configuration & DI registration

- Add `AiOptions` section to `appsettings.json` (model ID, endpoint, embedding model, chunk size, overlap, relevance threshold). **No API keys in config files** — use user-secrets / env vars / Key Vault.
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient(builder => builder.UseOpenAIChatClient(...))`.
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` via provider.
- Register `IVectorStore` / `VectorStoreCollection` via DI.
- Bind all settings with `IOptions<AiOptions>`.

### 3. Define the vector record model

- Create `DocumentChunk` record with:
  - `Key` (string, unique ID)
  - `Text` (string, chunk content)
  - `Embedding` (ReadOnlyMemory<float>, vector)
  - `Source` (string, original file path)
  - `Title` (string, document heading)
- Annotate with `[VectorStoreRecordKey]`, `[VectorStoreRecordData]`, `[VectorStoreRecordVector(Dimensions)]`.

### 4. Ingestion pipeline (`IngestionService`)

- Scan `Docs/` for `*.md` files.
- Parse each file, extract title from `# heading`.
- Chunk using semantic boundaries (split on `##` sections, then paragraphs). Use `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` for chunking. Ensure chunks have ~500 tokens with ~50 token overlap (configurable).
- Batch-embed chunks via `IEmbeddingGenerator` (batch calls to reduce latency/cost).
- Upsert into vector store collection. Cache embeddings — never re-embed unchanged content.
- Expose via `POST /ingest` endpoint (admin-only, returns chunk count).

### 5. Query endpoint (`QueryService`)

- `POST /ask` accepts `{ "question": "..." }`.
- Embed the question using the same `IEmbeddingGenerator`.
- Search vector store with `Top = 5` and `MinimumScore = 0.75f` (configurable).
- Build a grounded prompt:
  - System message: "Answer using only the provided context. Cite sources. If the context doesn't contain the answer, say so."
  - Inject retrieved chunks as context with source attribution.
  - User message: the original question.
- Call `IChatClient.GetResponseAsync()` with `Temperature = 0f`, `MaxOutputTokens = 1024`.
- Return `{ "answer": "...", "sources": [...] }` with source file references.

### 6. Guardrails to apply

| Guardrail | Implementation |
|-----------|---------------|
| Temperature = 0 | Factual Q&A — deterministic output preferred |
| MaxOutputTokens | Cap at 1024 to control cost |
| Relevance threshold | `MinimumScore = 0.75f` — don't inject irrelevant chunks |
| Source attribution | Track and return which chunks contributed |
| Retry logic | Wrap `IChatClient` with `RetryingChatClient` (3 retries, exponential backoff) |
| Token budget | Use `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` to count prompt tokens before sending; reject if over budget |
| Secret management | API keys via user-secrets (dev) / Key Vault (prod) |
| Model pinning | Pin to specific dated model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-2024-08-06`) |
| Health check | Add OpenAI health check to `/health` |
| Non-determinism handling | Validate LLM response shape; return graceful fallback if empty/malformed |
| Embedding caching | Store embeddings in vector store; skip re-embedding on re-ingest if content unchanged |

### 7. Project structure (new files)

```
ProductQA/
├── Controllers/
│   ├── IngestController.cs      # POST /ingest
│   └── AskController.cs         # POST /ask
├── Models/
│   ├── DocumentChunk.cs         # Vector store record
│   ├── AskRequest.cs            # { question }
│   └── AskResponse.cs           # { answer, sources[] }
├── Services/
│   ├── IngestionService.cs      # Chunking + embedding + upsert
│   └── QueryService.cs          # Retrieve + prompt + LLM call
├── Options/
│   └── AiOptions.cs             # Configuration POCO
├── Docs/
│   ├── configuration.md
│   └── getting-started.md
├── Program.cs                   # DI registration
└── ProductQA.csproj
```

### 8. Future enhancements (out of scope for v1)

- Swap in-memory vector store for Qdrant/pgvector in production
- Streaming responses via `IAsyncEnumerable` for lower time-to-first-token
- File-watcher to auto-re-ingest when docs change
- Semantic caching for repeated/similar questions
- Evaluation harness with golden Q&A dataset to measure answer quality over time

## Notes

- The in-memory vector store is fine for dev/testing with a small doc set (2 files currently). Production should use a persistent store.
- This is explicitly NOT an agentic scenario — no Microsoft.Agents.AI needed.
- All services go through MEAI abstractions — no direct provider SDK calls in business logic.

**Stack:** Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient` + `IEmbeddingGenerator`) with `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` for vector search — no Agent Framework needed since this is a simple retrieve-then-generate flow.

**Two endpoints:**

| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `POST /ingest` | Reads `Docs/*.md`, chunks by section/paragraph (~500 tokens), batch-embeds, upserts into vector store |
| `POST /ask` | Embeds the question, vector-searches (top 5, min score 0.75), builds grounded prompt, calls LLM, returns answer + source citations |

**Key packages:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`, `OpenAI`/`Azure.AI.OpenAI`, `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions`, `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` (preview), `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers`

**Guardrails:** Temperature=0, token budget enforcement, retry with backoff, relevance threshold filtering, source attribution, secrets via user-secrets/Key Vault, pinned model versions.

**Project layout:** Controllers, Services (`IngestionService`, `QueryService`), Models (`DocumentChunk`, DTOs), Options (`AiOptions`).
